system_summary.architecture_type: Layered monolith (single deployable WAR) with shared relational database
system_summary.deployment_model: On-prem Tomcat behind nginx, manual deployment via Jenkins (Jenkinsfile present)
system_summary.complexity_assessment: Medium-high: 14 Maven modules, 3 of them cyclically coupled through a shared `common` module; single PostgreSQL schema shared by all modules

assumptions: Production cannot be fully stopped; rolling deployment required
javax → jakarta namespace migration is mandatory for Boot 3
Team can allocate 2 engineers for the duration of the migration

upgrade_path.reason: Spring Boot 3 requires Java 17 minimum; 8 → 17 must land before the Boot 3 jump
Official migration guide requires passing through 2.7 (last 2.x) before 3.x
Target version; brings jakarta namespace, Spring Framework 6, Hibernate 6
Boot 3.3 runs on 17; 21 is the current LTS and the stated target runtime

breaking_changes.change: javax.* → jakarta.* namespace migration (Servlet, JPA, Validation, Annotation APIs)
breaking_changes.required_action: Rewrite imports across all modules; upgrade any third-party lib still shipping javax APIs

breaking_changes.change: WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter removed; SecurityFilterChain bean style required
breaking_changes.required_action: Rewrite security config to component-based lambda DSL
breaking_changes.automation_available: Partial via OpenRewrite spring-security recipes; manual review required

breaking_changes.change: Hibernate 6 changes sequence-based ID allocation and dialect resolution
breaking_changes.required_action: Verify generated SQL against the shared PostgreSQL schema before deploying; pin allocationSize explicitly
breaking_changes.automation_available: none — requires schema verification in staging

evidence: cyclic imports found in billing/src and orders/src

migration_plan.name: Baseline and safety net
migration_plan.objective: Make the current system safely upgradeable before touching any framework version
steps: Pin all Maven dependency versions currently resolved transitively (dependencyManagement)
Add integration tests around the 3 highest-traffic endpoints (evidence: no tests found in billing module)
Introduce API contract tests between billing and orders to expose the shared-table coupling
risks: Hidden behavior differences surface once tests are added
rollback_strategy: No production changes in this phase; revert branch only

migration_plan.name: Java 8 → 17 (LTS stepping stone)
migration_plan.objective: Upgrade language level without changing Spring Boot major version
steps: Upgrade to Java 17, fix removed-API usages (JAXB, javax.annotation found in common)
Run full regression in staging for one release cycle
risks: Reflection-heavy code in common module may break on newer JDK
rollback_strategy: Blue-green deploy; keep Java 8 artifacts published for one cycle

migration_plan.name: Spring Boot 3 + jakarta namespace
migration_plan.objective: Move to Boot 3.3 / Java 21 module by module, lowest-risk first
steps: Run OpenRewrite spring-boot-3 recipe on common, then auth-service, then remaining modules in priority order
Migrate Spring Security 5 → 6 (lambda DSL) in auth-service
Upgrade Hibernate 5.4 → 6.x; verify generated SQL against the shared schema before deploy
risks: Hibernate 6 changes ID generation and SQL dialect behavior against the shared database
rollback_strategy: Deploy per-module; previous WAR kept hot for instant rollback
Database untouched in this phase — schema changes deferred

critical_risks.risk: billing and orders share the `invoices` table directly
critical_risks.impact: Any schema or ORM change in one module can silently corrupt the other's writes
critical_risks.mitigation: Introduce a read-facade in phase 1 contract tests; defer schema ownership split until after Boot 3 is stable

quick_wins: Enable Maven build cache and parallel builds (build currently ~14 min per Jenkinsfile timeout settings)
Remove 6 unused dependencies flagged in common/pom.xml
unknowns_and_assumptions: unknown: production traffic volume and peak windows (no observability config found in repo)
unknown: whether external partners consume the SOAP endpoint in legacy-gateway (no consumer registry found)
assumption: PostgreSQL version is 12+ (inferred from docker-compose.yml, not confirmed for production)
